mission.title: Install and Configure k0s (Zero Friction Kubernetes)
mission.description: k0s is a lightweight, all-inclusive Kubernetes distribution designed for simplicity and ease of use. It is ideal for users looking to quickly bootstrap Kubernetes clusters on any infrastructure, including cloud, bare metal, and edge environments. The key trade-off is that while it simplifies installation, it may not offer the extensive customization options available in other distributions.

steps.title: Install the k0s binary
steps.description:
This step installs the k0s binary on your system, which is required to create and manage your Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

curl -sSL https://github.com/k0sproject/k0s/releases/download/v1.30.0+k0s.0/k0s-v1.30.0+k0s.0-linux-amd64 -o /usr/local/bin/k0s

chmod +x /usr/local/bin/k0s

```

> Download the k0s binary
> Make the binary executable

steps.title: Initialize the k0s controller
steps.description:
This command initializes the k0s controller, which will manage the Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

k0s init --single

```

> Creates a single-node cluster

steps.title: Start the k0s controller
steps.description:
This command starts the k0s controller to run your Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

k0s start

```

> Starts the k0s controller

steps.title: Verify the cluster is running
steps.description:
This command checks the status of the nodes in your cluster to ensure everything is functioning correctly.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> Verify that the node is in a Ready state

steps.title: Post-installation configuration
steps.description:
Configure additional features like autoscaling and monitoring as needed.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/k0sproject/k0s/master/examples/autoscaler.yaml

```

> Example for enabling autoscaling

uninstall.title: Uninstall k0s
uninstall.description:
This step will remove the k0s installation and all associated data. Be aware that this will delete your Kubernetes cluster and all resources within it.

```bash

k0s stop

rm -rf /var/lib/k0s

```

> Stop the k0s controller
> Remove all k0s data

upgrade.title: Upgrade k0s
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up your cluster data. This process will upgrade the k0s binary and restart the controller.

```bash

kubectl get nodes -o wide

curl -sSL https://github.com/k0sproject/k0s/releases/download/v1.31.0+k0s.0/k0s-v1.31.0+k0s.0-linux-amd64 -o /usr/local/bin/k0s

chmod +x /usr/local/bin/k0s

k0s upgrade

kubectl get nodes

```

> Backup current node status
> Download the new k0s binary
> Make the new binary executable
> Upgrade the k0s controller
> Verify nodes are in Ready state post-upgrade

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'k0s: command not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The k0s binary is not installed or not in the PATH.

**Fix:**
Ensure the k0s binary is installed and located in /usr/local/bin.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'kubelet not running'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The k0s controller may not have started correctly.

**Fix:**
Run 'k0s start' to start the k0s controller.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'kubectl get nodes' returns no nodes
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The cluster may not be initialized or the controller is not running.

**Fix:**
Run 'k0s init --single' and 'k0s start'.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'k0s upgrade' fails with version mismatch
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The k0s binary version does not match the cluster version.

**Fix:**
Ensure the k0s binary is the same version as the cluster.

**Affected versions:** <= 1.30

versionNotes.changes: Added support for multi-cluster management, improved logging features.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated the old CLI flags for cluster management.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: Run 'k0s upgrade' to migrate from previous versions.

resolution.summary: You should see output from 'kubectl get nodes' showing your node in a Ready state.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the required tools and resources before starting the installation.
